DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-05-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DOE 1'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Doe 1's demurrer to the complaint's one cause of action, negligence, is overruled.
Defendants are a nonprofit (Doe 1) and its local affiliate (Doe 2) that provided mentors to disadvantaged youth such as plaintiff. In the early 1980s, Elias Ramos was placed as plaintiff's older brother's mentor. (Cmplt. 7:26-8:6.) After plaintiff's original mentor moved away, Ramos became plaintiff's "unofficial" mentor as well. (Id.) The complaint pleads that Ramos spent the next three years sexually molesting plaintiff at least weekly, until plaintiff's mother caught Ramos in the act when plaintiff was 12. (Id. at 9:2-13.)
Doe 1 has three arguments.

First, it says it had "no duty" to "protect plaintiff" from "Ramos' criminal conduct." (Memo. 4:16-18.) However, plaintiff adequately pleads a "special relationship" based on Doe 1's recruiting, screening and training of Ramos as a mentor in its program, as well as its continued supervision and control of him while he molested plaintiff. (Cmplt. 3:9-19, 6:10-14:28.)

Second, Doe 1 claims the complaint lacks "specific allegations to meet the heightened pleading standard to prevent a third party crime." (Memo. 7:9-11.) The argument incorrectly presumes that Ramos is pled to be a "third party" unrelated to defendants. Instead, as shown above, the complaint specifically alleges that defendants introduced Ramos into plaintiff's home after recruiting, screening and training him and then continued to supervise and control him as he molested plaintiff.

Third, Doe 1 says it "cannot be held vicariously liable for Ramos' assault on plaintiff." (Memo. 8:8-9.) The complaint adequately pleads agency. (Id. at 3:9-19, 4:2-11.) In any event, this argument fails because a "demurrer does not lie to a portion of a cause of action" and other negligence theories are pled. (Elder v. Pacific Bell Telephone Co. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 841, 856 n.14.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-05-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DOE 1'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Doe 1's motion to strike is denied.

Defendants are a nonprofit (Doe 1) and its local affiliate (Doe 2) that provided mentors to disadvantaged youth such as plaintiff. In the early 1980s, Elias Ramos was placed as plaintiff's older brother's mentor. (Cmplt. 7:26-8:6.) After plaintiff's original mentor moved away, Ramos became plaintiff's "unofficial" mentor as well. (Id.) The complaint pleads that Ramos spent the next three years sexually molesting plaintiff at least weekly, until plaintiff's mother caught Ramos in the act when plaintiff was 12. (Id. at 9:2-13.)

Punitive Damages. Doe 1 first seeks to strike punitive damage allegations. (Memo. 4:22-7:17.) However, such allegations are specifically pled, including against the corporations. (See, e.g., Cmplt. 4:27-5:15, 4:19-26, 2:28-3:19, 6:24-26, 7:5-10, 7:26-8:6, 10:8-12, 10:24-11:2.) A complaint need not recite Civil Code 3294 verbatim, as Doe 1 suggests. (See Memo. 5:10-20.) Nor must a plaintiff "prove" punitive damages at the pleading stage. (See id. at 5:21-24.) Finally, incanting the word "conclusory" does not make a complaint so. (See id at 6:26-7:6.)

"False Allegations." Doe 1 also seeks to strike from the complaint allegations it deems "false." (Memo. 7:18-9:18.) At the pleading stage, a plaintiff's allegations must be accepted as true. (Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Corp. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604.)

Criminal Record. Relying on its inaccurate notion that Ramos is pled to be an unrelated "third party," Doe 1 would strike a paragraph regarding his subsequent criminal record of sexual assaults on minors. (Memo. 9:9-10:7.) However, Ramos' pedophilic proclivities may well be relevant to, inter alia, Doe 1's screening of its mentors.

Statutory Violations. Doe 1 finds more allegations it deems "false" in a paragraph alleging statutory violations. (Memo. 10:8-11:14.) Again, a plaintiff's allegations must be accepted as true at the pleading stage. (Del E. Webb,123 Cal.App.3d at 604.) Moreover, "use of the motion to strike should be cautious and sparing," not "a procedural 'line item veto' for the civil defendant." (PH II. Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1680, 1683.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
